Cao Mao: Martyr or Monster?

	Date: 03/2013
	Replies: 10
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cao Mao was a Cao Wei prince who was the Prince of Laoling, the son of Cao Cao. His haughty behavior made him despised by his imperial relatives, and throughout Cao Wei’s existence, he rarely received any favor. He has not mentioned having died in extant records, and it is unknown whether he survived to Western Jin.

Cao Mao was born to Cao Cao and Lady Zhao. It is claimed by Cao Rui in an edict that Cao Mao was ignorant of common courtesy when he was young, and as he grew older, he did not act virtuously. He was also known for being defiant and arrogant. These traits made him unfavored by Cao Cao, and his older brother, the Emperor Cao Pi, did not even make him a Prince during his reign. One of Cao Mao’s notorious deeds was when his brother, Cao Hui, died, he faked a throat illness to not mourn. However, he was caught living casually, and there were high ranking officials who petitioned to punish him by removal of land. Cao Mao was stripped of one county.

Later, because he had low food income and the fact that he fathered many children, his stripped lands were reinstated. Throughout Cao Wei’s existence, Cao Mao was raised to a measly total of 700 households.

Only one of his sons is named: Cao Song.

full_text:
Cao Mao 曹茂
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Mao (曹茂). Arrogant and vicious, he had to wait a long time to become prince. Had a large family. Cao Mao was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom.
